title: Pseudodictamnus africanus
taxonomy: Pseudodictamnus africanus is classified under the family Boraginaceae, a family of flowering plants. This plant is a member of the genus Pseudodictamnus, which is a small group of flowering plants. The Boraginaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including species such as forget-me-nots and borage. Pseudodictamnus africanus is native to tropical Africa and is found in a variety of habitats. The classification of Pseudodictamnus africanus is still a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
uses: Pseudodictamnus africanus is a plant species that has been traditionally used in some regions for its medicinal properties.
